fields.description:
## Why
The record/fold path (S2) and read surface (S3) handle a single opaque session id, but a
bare session id collides across tools (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Cursor). To make the
feature genuinely multi-harness, claims must also record WHICH harness produced the id, and
the resolver must recognise the other OSS harness session vars.

## What
- **Resolver var-list extension (`src/rebar/_commands/session_id.py`).** Extend
  `_SESSION_ID_VARS` with the OSS harness var OpenCode ships, in popularity order between the
  Claude and ambient vars:
  `REBAR_SESSION_ID -> CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> OPENCODE_SESSION_ID -> SESSION_ID`.
  **Codex is deliberately NOT added as a native var:** story 7656 establishes that Codex
  exposes no supported readable session env var, so it is covered by its SessionStart shim
  exporting `REBAR_SESSION_ID` (not a `CODEX_THREAD_ID`). SAFETY: an absent or unrecognised var
  simply falls through (the resolver skips unset vars), so it degrades to a lower-precedence
  var or `None` — never an error.
- **Harness provenance tag.** Add `resolve_harness()` returning the opaque, rebar-owned
  `AI_AGENT` env var (verbatim, never interpolated) or `None`. `AI_AGENT` is rebar's
  harness-provenance convention, holding e.g. `claude-code_<ver>` / `opencode` / `codex` /
  `cursor`. **Population contract (closes the DOA gap):** the capture shims in stories S4
  (ec5c) and S6 (7656) are extended by THIS epic to export `AI_AGENT` alongside
  `REBAR_SESSION_ID`; until then `AI_AGENT` may also be set directly by an operator/hook. When
  unset, `claim_harness` folds to `None` (no error) — the feature degrades gracefully.
  On an `open -> in_progress` STATUS, stamp `data["harness"]` when present; fold to
  `state["claim_harness"]`. Each provenance field folds `data.get(<its key>)` INDEPENDENTLY on
  the edge (same fork-winner gating as `claimed_session`), so a re-claim clears `claim_harness`
  to `None` iff `AI_AGENT` is absent for THAT claim — independent of whether a session id is
  present.
- **Secondary remote session.** Add `resolve_remote_session()` returning
  `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID` or `None`; stamp `data["remote_session"]` and fold to
  `state["claim_remote_session"]` (same gating). This is a secondary field, independent of the
  primary `claimed_session`.
- **State + schema + read surface.** Default `claim_harness` and `claim_remote_session` to
  `None` in `make_initial_state`; enumerate both in `ticket_state.schema.json` and (as compact
  keys `chn` / `rsn`) in `ticket_state_llm.schema.json` + `KEY_MAP` (+ docstring); add both to
  the MCP `TicketStateOut` model; regenerate `types.py`. `rebar show` JSON includes them
  automatically (state keys).
- **Existing-test updates.** Update `tests/unit/test_session_id.py` for the extended list:
  broaden its hardcoded `_ALL_VARS` tuple + autouse clear fixture to the 4 vars, and update
  `test_docs_document_unified_contract`'s exact-string assertion to the extended ordered
  contract (normalising whitespace so doc line-wrapping is irrelevant). Add an
  `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` precedence case, and a case asserting `CODEX_THREAD_ID` is NOT
  recognised (Codex has no native var).
- **Docs.** Extend `docs/config.md` (the ordered var list + `AI_AGENT` +
  `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID`) and `docs/event-schema.md` (the `harness` / `remote_session`
  data keys -> `claim_harness` / `claim_remote_session` state). Env-var name provenance:
  `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` (OpenCode-shipped) and `CODEX_THREAD_ID` (Codex convention) come from
  the epic OSS survey (Codex has no readable var, hence no `CODEX_THREAD_ID`); because an
  unrecognised name simply falls through, a wrong guess degrades to a lower-precedence var or
  `None` with no runtime error.

## Scope
- IN: resolver var-list extension + `resolve_harness` / `resolve_remote_session`; the write
  stamps + reducer folds for `claim_harness` / `claim_remote_session`; state defaults; both
  JSON schemas + `types.py` regen + `to_llm` keys + MCP model; docs; the existing
  `test_session_id.py` updates AND a NEW `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` module.
- OUT: the per-harness CAPTURE shims that POPULATE `REBAR_SESSION_ID` / `AI_AGENT` — Claude
  Code (S4 / ec5c) and Codex + Cursor (S6 / 7656), both extended by this epic to also export
  `AI_AGENT`. This story is the store/record side only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `resolve_session_id()` recognises `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` at its precedence, and does NOT
      recognise `CODEX_THREAD_ID` (Codex has no native var). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_session_id.py -k "opencode or codex" -q`.
- [ ] A claim records `claim_harness` (from `AI_AGENT`) in compiled state, distinguishing the
      source tool; absent `AI_AGENT` -> `None`. Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k harness -q`.
- [ ] `CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_SESSION_ID`, when set, is captured as `claim_remote_session`. Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k remote -q`.
- [ ] Both fields default to `None` in `make_initial_state`, are enumerated in
      `ticket_state.schema.json` + `ticket_state_llm.schema.json` (`chn` / `rsn`), and appear
      in `TicketStateOut`; `types.py` is not stale. Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k "schema or read" -q` and
      `pytest tests/interfaces/contracts/test_public_types.py -q`.
- [ ] Fork-winner + session-less-clear semantics apply to both new fields (never the loser's;
      cleared to `None` on a provenance-less re-claim). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k fork -q`.
- [ ] `docs/config.md` and `docs/event-schema.md` document the extended var list, `AI_AGENT`,
      and the two new fields. Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py -k docs -q`
      asserts `docs/config.md` contains `AI_AGENT` and `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID` (Codex has no
      readable var, so there is intentionally no `CODEX_THREAD_ID`), AND `docs/event-schema.md`
      contains `claim_harness` and `claim_remote_session`.
- [ ] The existing `test_session_id.py` is updated for the extended list and stays green
      (4-var `_ALL_VARS` + clear fixture; unified-contract doc assertion updated). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_session_id.py -q`.
- [ ] A NEW `tests/unit/test_harness_provenance.py` module is created covering the resolvers,
      write-stamp/fold, fork-winner + provenance-less-clear, schemas + read surface, and docs.

## Depends on
- S2 (68ef) record/fold path + S3 (199b) read surface — landed in this stack.
